html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 右台仙馆 - A name for the author's retreat, which can be translated as 'Right Terrace Immortal Pavilion.'
  • 顏之曰 - Means 'named it as' or 'called it.'
  • 曲園 - The name of the author's garden in Suzhou, which can be translated as 'Curve Garden.'
  • 《曲園雜纂》 - A collection of writings from the Curve Garden, which can be translated as 'Miscellaneous Writings of the Curve Garden.'
  • 俞樓 - The name of the author's residence in Hangzhou, which can be translated as 'Yu Pavilion.'
  • 《俞樓雜纂》 - A collection of writings from the Yu Pavilion, which can be translated as 'Miscellaneous Writings of the Yu Pavilion.'
  • 筆記者 - Refers to notes or records, which can be translated as 'note-taker' or 'recorder.'
  • 雜記 - Means 'miscellaneous records' or 'random notes.'
  • 搜神 - Refers to the collection 'Search for the Supernatural,' which can be translated as 'In Search of the Supernatural.'
  • 述異 - Refers to the collection 'Records of the Strange,' which can be translated as 'Records of the Unusual.'
  • 不古訓之是式 - Means 'not adhering to ancient teachings as a model.'
  • 怪之欲聞 - Means 'desiring to hear the strange.'
  • 志荒 - Means 'mind wandering' or 'thoughts becoming scattered.'
  • 曲園居士 - A self-reference, which can be translated as 'Buddhist Hermit of the Curve Garden.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 右台山 - Located in Hangzhou, known for its scenic beauty and historical significance.
  • 錢唐 - An ancient name for Hangzhou.
  • 姚夫人 - The author's wife, who passed away in the year mentioned.
  • 吳下 - Refers to the region around Suzhou, where the author lived.
  • 俞樓 - The author's residence in Hangzhou, named after himself.
  • 右台仙馆 - The author's retreat, named after his wife's burial site.
  • 冠蓋 - Refers to officialdom or high-ranking officials.
  • 松楸 - Refers to cypress trees, often associated with graves and mourning.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence describes the author's declining mental and physical state after the death of his wife.
  • This sentence describes the burial of the author's wife and the author's own preparations for his grave.
  • This sentence describes the construction of the author's retreat.
  • This sentence describes the author's lifestyle at his retreat.
  • This sentence draws a parallel between the author's other residences and their respective collections of writings.
  • This sentence explains why the author is collecting his notes.
  • This sentence defines what the author's notes consist of.
  • This sentence expresses the author's state of mind regarding his notes.
  • This sentence concludes that these notes are the writings of the Right Terrace Immortal Pavilion.
  • This sentence identifies the author as the Buddhist Hermit of the Curve Garden.

Proper Nouns

  • 俞樾 - The author of the text.
  • 姚夫人 - The author's wife.
  • 右台山 - A mountain in Hangzhou.
  • 钱唐 - An ancient name for Hangzhou.
  • 吴下 - The region around Suzhou.
  • 俞楼 - The author's residence in Hangzhou.
  • 右台仙馆 - The author's retreat.
  • 曲园 - The author's garden in Suzhou.
  • 《曲园杂纂》 - A collection of writings from the Curve Garden.
  • 《俞楼杂纂》 - A collection of writings from the Yu Pavilion.
  • 曲园居士 - A self-reference by the author.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 附錄 - Means 'appendix' or 'supplement', indicating that this is an additional piece of writing.
  • 征求异闻启 - A compound title where '征求' means 'to solicit' or 'to seek', '异闻' means 'strange or unusual events', and '启' means 'letter' or 'announcement'. Together, it refers to a letter inviting others to share strange or unusual stories.
  • 学问之道 - Literally 'the way of learning', referring to the pursuit of knowledge.
  • 日就荒芜 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'gradually becoming neglected or abandoned', often used to describe the decline of something over time.
  • 著述之事 - Refers to the act of writing or composing works.
  • 行将废辍 - Means 'about to be abandoned' or 'on the verge of being discontinued'.
  • 书生结习 - Literally 'the habits of a scholar', referring to the lingering tendencies or inclinations of a scholar.
  • 未能尽忘 - Means 'cannot be completely forgotten'.
  • 姑记旧闻 - Means 'temporarily record old stories' or 'temporarily note down past events'.
  • 销暇日 - Means 'to pass the leisure time' or 'to fill the idle hours'.
  • 所闻所见 - Refers to 'what is heard and seen'.
  • 集腋而成 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to gather small pieces to form a whole', often used to describe the process of collecting information or materials.
  • 予取予求 - Means 'to take what is needed' or 'to seek what is desired'.
  • 乞邻之意 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'the intention to borrow from neighbors', often used to describe the act of seeking help or assistance.
  • 儒林丈人 - Refers to 'scholars' or 'learned men', often used to address respected scholars.
  • 高斋学士 - Refers to 'scholars in high places' or 'distinguished scholars', often used to address respected academics.
  • 各举怪怪奇奇之事 - Means 'each person should report strange and unusual events'.
  • 为我原原本本而书 - Means 'to write down the details in full' or 'to provide a detailed account'.
  • 寄来“春在草堂” - Means 'send to 'Spring in the Thatched Hut'', referring to the recipient's residence or place of work.
  • 助作秋灯丛话 - Means 'to help compile 'Autumn Lamp Collection of Stories'', referring to the compilation of stories.
  • 约以十事为率 - Means 'to aim for ten items as a guideline' or 'to target ten items as a standard'.
  • 如其多则更佳 - Means 'if more, it would be even better'.
  • 先将二绝为媒 - Means 'first, send two short poems as a prelude' or 'first, send two couplets as an introduction'.
  • 幸勿置之不答 - Means 'please do not ignore it' or 'please do not disregard it'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 儒林丈人 - Refers to respected scholars, often used in classical Chinese literature to address learned individuals.
  • 高斋学士 - Refers to distinguished scholars, often used in classical Chinese literature to address respected academics.
  • 春在草堂 - Refers to the recipient's residence or place of work, often used in classical Chinese literature to denote a scholar's retreat or study.
  • 秋灯丛话 - Refers to the compilation of stories, often used in classical Chinese literature to denote a collection of tales or anecdotes.

Proper Nouns

  • 儒林丈人 - Respected scholars.
  • 高斋学士 - Distinguished scholars.
  • 春在草堂 - The recipient's residence or place of work.
  • 秋灯丛话 - The compilation of stories.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 衰不复事丹铅 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'in a declining state, no longer engages in scholarly pursuits.'
  • 六十原非亲学年 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'originally not a year for close study.'
  • 正似东坡老无事 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'just like Su Dongpo, old and idle.'
  • 听人说鬼便欣然 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'listen to people tell ghost stories and gladly.'
  • 郭冲五事太寥寥 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'the five events related to Guo Chong are too few.'
  • 戏学姚崇十事要 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'playfully learn the ten important events related to Yao Chong.'
  • 不论搜神兼志怪 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'regardless of seeking the divine and recording strange events.'
  • 妄言亦可慰无聊 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'idle talk can also comfort boredom.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 衰 - Refers to someone in a declining state, possibly old age.
  • 丹铅 - Refers to scholarly pursuits, particularly reading and writing.
  • 东坡 - Refers to Su Dongpo (Su Shi), a famous poet and statesman from the Song Dynasty.
  • 郭冲 - A historical figure, though specific details about him are not widely known.
  • 姚崇 - A famous statesman from the Tang Dynasty, known for his political reforms and achievements.
  • 搜神 - Refers to seeking the divine or supernatural.
  • 志怪 - Refers to recording strange or supernatural events.

Proper Nouns

  • 东坡 - Su Dongpo (Su Shi), a famous poet and statesman from the Song Dynasty.
  • 郭冲 - A historical figure, though specific details about him are not widely known.
  • 姚崇 - A famous statesman from the Tang Dynasty, known for his political reforms and achievements.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 年甫四岁 - Just four years old; indicates the age of Tao when the event occurred.
  • 解腰缠出银数锭 - Unfastening the belt and taking out several pieces of silver; describes the old man's possessions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金陵 - Ancient name for Nanjing, a significant city in Chinese history.
  • 粤贼 - Guangxi bandits; refers to the Taiping rebels during the Taiping Rebellion.
  • 同治甲戌 - Year Jia Xu of the Tongzhi era; a specific year in the late Qing Dynasty.
  • 瞽妇 - Blind woman; indicates a person with visual impairment.
  • 续娶 - Second wife; refers to a remarriage after the death of the first spouse.
  • 阴德 - Secret virtue; a concept in Chinese culture that refers to good deeds done without seeking recognition.

Proper Nouns

  • 木工 - Woodworker; a common profession in traditional Chinese society.
  • 金陵 - Ancient name for Nanjing.
  • 粤贼 - Guangxi bandits; referring to the Taiping rebels.
  • 同治甲戌 - Year Jia Xu of the Tongzhi era.
  • 瞽妇 - Blind woman.
  • 续娶 - Second wife.
  • 阴德 - Secret virtue.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吾兄福宁太守 - Refers to the narrator's elder brother, the Prefect of Fuzhou.
  • 署附近有僧寺曰“碧山岩寺” - Near the government office, there is a Buddhist temple called 'Biyanshan Rock Temple.'
  • 能诗画，貌亦恂恂，吐属风雅 - He could compose poetry and paint, and his appearance was also modest and refined, with elegant speech.
  • 具牒请还俗 - He presented a petition requesting to return to secular life.
  • 呵叱而遣之 - He scolded and dismissed him.
  • 故与署中吏魁某甲有隙 - He had a grudge against a certain official, Mr. A, in the government office.
  • 疑其谮于官也 - He suspected that Mr. A had slandered him to the official.
  • 经由一隘巷 - He passed through a narrow alley.
  • 闻碧禅呼其名，回顾之 - He heard the sound of Mr. A calling his name and turned back.
  • 则已事刂刃于胸出于背 - And then he was stabbed through the chest, the blade coming out from his back.
  • 犹能带刃狂奔，至家，告家人曰：“碧禅杀我。”言已，倒地而绝 - He was still able to run wildly with the blade in him, returning home, and told his family, 'Mr. A killed me.' After saying this, he fell to the ground and died.
  • 有人于署后山上见碧禅，则已有发辫矣 - Later, someone saw Mr. A on the hill behind the government office, and he already had his hair braided.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 福宁太守 - Refers to the position of Prefect of Fuzhou, a high-ranking official in the Qing dynasty.
  • 碧山岩寺 - A specific Buddhist temple located near the government office, which plays a central role in the narrative.
  • 碧禅 - A Buddhist monk who later returns to secular life, becoming Mr. A.
  • 署中潘少梅、蔡瑜卿诸君 - Refers to several officials or scholars in the government office, including Pan Shaomei and Cai Yuqing, who are known for their literary and scholarly pursuits.
  • 还俗 - The act of a monk returning to secular life, which was a significant event in traditional Chinese society.
  • 署后山 - Refers to the hill behind the government office, where Mr. A is later found.

Proper Nouns

  • 吾兄 - Refers to the narrator's elder brother, who holds the position of Prefect of Fuzhou.
  • 福宁 - The name of the prefecture where the story takes place.
  • 碧山岩寺 - The name of the Buddhist temple near the government office.
  • 碧禅 - The name of the monk who later becomes Mr. A.
  • 潘少梅 - The name of an official or scholar in the government office.
  • 蔡瑜卿 - The name of another official or scholar in the government office.
  • 某甲 - A placeholder name for the official who harbored a grudge against the monk.
  • 署 - The government office or residence of the official.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 呼曰 - Means 'shouted' or 'called out.'
  • 碧禅在此 - A phrase used by the character to announce their presence.
  • 蹴其人仆地 - Means 'kicked the person to the ground.'
  • 捷如飞鸟 - A simile meaning 'as swift as a flying bird.'
  • 帽坠于地 - Means 'the hat fell to the ground.'
  • 辫乃缀于帽 - Means 'the braid was attached to the hat.'
  • 非真发也 - Means 'not real hair.'
  • 嗣后 - Means 'subsequently' or 'thereafter.'
  • 幕友 - Refers to a private secretary or advisor.
  • 房中每夜窗户不开 - Means 'every night in his room, the windows were not opened.'
  • 频有所失 - Means 'frequently lost things.'
  • 得之署后山穴中 - Means 'found in the cave behind the office building.'
  • 疑碧禅所为也 - Means 'suspected that it was done by the Blue Chan.'
  • 捕事益懈 - Means 'the pursuit became more lax.'
  • 还省 - Means 'return to the provincial capital.'
  • 鼓山 - Refers to a mountain, likely a specific location.
  • 大骇 - Means 'greatly shocked.'
  • 不知所自来 - Means 'did not know where it came from.'
  • 古剑客之流 - Means 'a type of ancient swordsmen.'
  • 不复言捕碧禅 - Means 'no longer spoke of capturing the Blue Chan.'
  • 任满 - Means 'the term of office ended.'
  • 不敢至鼓山 - Means 'dared not go to the Drum Mountain.'
  • 始终不可得 - Means 'could never be found.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 碧禅 - A name or title, possibly referring to a character with special abilities or a mythical figure.
  • 福防同知 - A title, likely referring to a high-ranking official.
  • 龚司马 - A title, likely referring to a high-ranking official.
  • 捕 - Refers to the act of capturing or arresting.
  • 鼓山 - A specific mountain, possibly a significant location in the story.
  • 古剑客 - Refers to ancient swordsmen, often associated with legendary figures in Chinese literature.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence describes a series of actions performed by the Blue Chan, including kicking someone to the ground and leaping down from the mountain.
  • This sentence describes the appearance of the Blue Chan, noting the hat and the braid.
  • This sentence describes the subsequent events involving a private secretary named Wang, who frequently lost items.
  • This sentence explains that the lost items were found in a cave behind the office building, leading to suspicion that the Blue Chan was responsible.
  • This sentence describes the passage of time and the decreasing frequency of the lost items.
  • This sentence describes the change in officials and the new order to capture the Blue Chan.
  • This sentence describes the efforts to find the Blue Chan, leading to a confrontation.
  • This sentence describes the Blue Chan's escape.
  • This sentence describes the discovery of a letter in the inner room.
  • This sentence is the content of the letter, explaining the Blue Chan's motives.
  • This sentence is a warning or threat.
  • This sentence describes the reaction to the letter and the suspicion about the Blue Chan's identity.
  • This sentence describes the decision not to pursue the Blue Chan further.
  • This sentence describes the final outcome and the Blue Chan's continued elusiveness.

Proper Nouns

  • 碧禅 - A name or title.
  • 福防同知 - A title.
  • 龚司马 - A title.
  • 鼓山 - A specific mountain.
  • 古剑客 - A type of character.
  • 金姓者 - A person named Jin.
  • 妓楼 - A brothel.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大家风范 - Refers to the manners and behaviors typical of wealthy families.
  • 修眉纤趾 - Describes beautiful eyebrows and slender toes.
  • 烹饪缝纫 - Highlights skills in cooking and sewing.
  • 诵唐人小诗 - Refers to reciting small poems from the Tang dynasty.
  • 及笄 - A traditional Chinese term for a girl reaching the age of marriage, typically around 15 years old.
  • 井臼 - Refers to household chores, particularly those involving water and food preparation.
  • 大妇 - Refers to the official's primary wife, who held a higher status than a concubine.
  • 室 - An archaic term for a concubine, indicating a secondary wife.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 农家女 - Refers to a woman from a farming family, reflecting the social status of the time.
  • 唐人小诗 - Refers to short poems from the Tang dynasty, a period known for its rich literary tradition.
  • 田舍儿 - Refers to a farmer's son, indicating a lower social status.
  • 富家子 - Refers to a young man from a wealthy family, indicating a higher social status.
  • 官人为妾 - Refers to a concubine of an official, a common practice in feudal China where officials could have multiple wives and concubines.
  • 黄大令 - Refers to a magistrate named Huang, a local government official.

Proper Nouns

  • 邢阿金 - The name of the subject, a farmer's daughter.
  • 黄大令 - The name of a magistrate, a local government official.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 謀以為妻 - To plan to take her as a wife, indicating a serious intention to marry.
  • 出身微贱 - Born of lowly origins, describing someone of humble background.
  • 伉俪君子 - A match for a gentleman, used to describe a suitable partner for a man of high moral standing.
  • 损折年寿 - To shorten one's lifespan, indicating the belief that marrying a person of low status could be harmful.
  • 累君盛德 - To tarnish your noble virtues, suggesting that the marriage could bring shame to the husband's good name.
  • 鉴其诚 - Recognized her sincerity, indicating that the husband saw through her genuine feelings.
  • 益爱重之 - Loved and respected her even more, showing increased affection and respect.
  • 四易所天 - Changed husbands four times, indicating multiple remarriages.
  • 不为贞 - Not considered chaste, suggesting that she did not maintain the traditional virtue of chastity.
  • 卒殉其夫 - Ultimately sacrificed herself for her husband, indicating her ultimate act of loyalty and devotion.
  • 附葬如礼 - Buried according to proper rites, indicating that she was buried with the appropriate ceremonies.
  • 质美而未学 - Of noble nature but lacking in education, suggesting that she had good qualities but was not well-educated.
  • 君子哀其遇 - Gentlemen mourn her fate, indicating sympathy for her difficult life.
  • 取其晚盖 - Respect her later virtue, suggesting that her later actions were worthy of admiration.
  • 豫让“众人”、“国士”之言 - The words of Yu Rang about 'common people' and 'national heroes', a reference to a famous historical figure and his views on different levels of loyalty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黄妻久卒 - Yellow's wife had passed away long ago, a reference to a deceased wife.
  • 阿金不可 - Ah Jin refused, a reference to a woman named Ah Jin.
  • 黄鉴其诚 - Yellow recognized her sincerity, a reference to a man named Yellow.
  • 黄有子妇 - Yellow had a daughter-in-law, a reference to a daughter-in-law.
  • 年相若 - Of similar age, indicating that the daughter-in-law was around the same age as Ah Jin.
  • 子妇承翁意 - The daughter-in-law followed the wishes of her father-in-law, indicating respect for the father-in-law's wishes.
  • 事之如姑 - Treated her as if she were a mother-in-law, indicating respectful treatment.
  • 阿金虽谦不敢当 - Ah Jin, although humble, dared not accept, indicating modesty.
  • 然子妇执礼不衰 - But the daughter-in-law continued to observe the proper etiquette, indicating continued respect.
  • 黄病 - Yellow fell ill, a reference to the husband's illness.
  • 阿金侍汤药惟谨 - Ah Jin served him with soup and medicine with great care, indicating attentive care.
  • 竟仰药以殉 - Ultimately drank poison to die with him, indicating her ultimate sacrifice.
  • 黄之子感其殉父 - Yellow's son was moved by her sacrifice for his father, indicating the son's emotional response.
  • 附葬如礼焉 - Buried according to proper rites, indicating the proper burial ceremony.
  • 豫让“众人”、“国士”之言 - The words of Yu Rang about 'common people' and 'national heroes', a reference to a famous historical figure and his views on different levels of loyalty.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄 - A man's name, likely referring to a historical or fictional character.
  • 阿金 - A woman's name, likely referring to a historical or fictional character.
  • 豫让 - A famous historical figure known for his loyalty and sacrifice.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 戒行颇高 - Refers to highly virtuous conduct, common in descriptions of Buddhist monks.
  • 皮毛脱落 - Describes the physical symptoms of leprosy, specifically the loss of skin and fur.
  • 一闻木鱼声，辄摇尾而至 - Describes the dog's behavior of wagging its tail and coming as soon as it hears the sound of the wooden fish, a traditional Buddhist instrument used during chanting.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 江西玉山县 - A specific location in Jiangxi Province, China, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 古刹 - Refers to an ancient temple, a common cultural landmark in Chinese history.
  • 老僧曰月印 - The name 'Yue Yin' is a proper noun, referring to an old monk, highlighting the importance of names in religious contexts.
  • 忽病癞 - Leprosy is a historical disease that has been documented in Chinese history, reflecting the cultural understanding of the disease.
  • 皮毛脱落 - Describes the physical symptoms of leprosy, reflecting the cultural understanding of the disease's impact.
  • 且有秽气 - Describes the unpleasant smell associated with leprosy, reflecting the cultural perception of the disease's effects.
  • 然每日听经如故 - Reflects the dog's continued devotion despite its condition, highlighting the spiritual and religious practices in a Buddhist temple.
  • 月印忽谓其徒曰 - Reflects the authority and respect given to the master in a monastic setting, emphasizing the hierarchical structure of religious institutions.
  • 此狗可厌 - Reflects the master's frustration with the dog's condition, showing the emotional and practical challenges faced by religious figures.
  • 汝曹可牵出扑杀之 - Reflects the strict discipline and rules in a monastic setting, highlighting the importance of obedience and order.
  • 其徒咸骇然 - Reflects the disciples' shock and confusion, showing the emotional impact of the master's command.
  • 罔测其意 - Reflects the disciples' inability to understand the master's intentions, emphasizing the complexity of religious authority.
  • 以师素严重 - Reflects the master's strict and serious demeanor, highlighting the respect and fear accorded to religious leaders.
  • 不敢违 - Reflects the disciples' obedience to the master, emphasizing the hierarchical structure of religious institutions.
  • 姑呼狗出 - Reflects the temporary nature of the action, showing the practical considerations in religious settings.
  • 禁勿使听经 - Reflects the master's decision to stop the dog from listening to scriptures, highlighting the importance of spiritual practices.
  • 实未忍杀之也 - Reflects the disciples' compassion and reluctance to harm the dog, showing the emotional and ethical considerations in religious settings.

Proper Nouns

  • 江西玉山县 - Yushan County in Jiangxi Province, China, a specific location with historical and cultural significance.
  • 水南寺 - A specific temple in Yushan County, known for its historical and cultural importance.
  • 老僧曰月印 - An old monk named Yue Yin, a proper noun referring to a specific religious figure.
  • 汝曹 - A term used to address a group of people, in this case, the disciples, reflecting the hierarchical structure of religious institutions.
  • 木鱼 - A traditional Buddhist instrument used during chanting, reflecting the cultural and religious practices in a Buddhist temple.

[10]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 越三日 - Temporal expression meaning 'three days later.'
  • 乘间 - Means 'taking advantage of an opportunity' or 'seizing a moment.'
  • 聽诵经 - Means 'listening to the recitation of scriptures.'
  • 月印见之 - '月印' is a person's name, likely a Buddhist monk or master, and '见之' means 'saw it.'
  • 惊曰 - Means 'exclaimed in surprise.'
  • 汝曹未杀此犬乎 - '汝曹' is an archaic term for 'you all' or 'you people,' and '未杀此犬乎' means 'haven't you killed this dog yet?'
  • 殆矣，殆矣 - A repetitive phrase expressing deep concern or warning, similar to 'it's dangerous, it's dangerous.'
  • 命其徒至某村某姓家探问 - '命其徒' means 'ordered his disciples,' '至某村某姓家' means 'to a certain village and a certain family,' and '探问' means 'to inquire.'
  • 坐蓐 - Means 'in labor' or 'giving birth.'
  • 三日未产 - Means 'three days without giving birth.'
  • 其势垂危 - Means 'the situation is critical.'
  • 乳医束手 - '乳医' refers to a midwife or doctor specializing in childbirth, and '束手' means 'helpless' or 'at a loss.'
  • 汝曹不忍杀此犬，乃忍杀彼妇乎 - '汝曹不忍杀此犬' means 'you all can't bear to kill this dog,' and '乃忍杀彼妇乎' means 'but can you bear to kill that woman?'
  • 此犬不死，彼妇不产 - Means 'if this dog doesn't die, that woman won't give birth.'
  • 命其徒立时扑杀之 - '命其徒' means 'ordered his disciples,' '立时' means 'immediately,' and '扑杀之' means 'to strike and kill it.'
  • 再侦之 - Means 'to check again.'

  • 因情而死可也，谓之守义而死亦可也 - It can be called dying out of emotion, or it can also be called dying out of loyalty. Presents two possible interpretations of the same event.
  • 固非此事所能拟矣 - It certainly cannot be compared to this matter. Emphasizes the uniqueness of the current situation.
  • 沪俗伪鬻妻妾于人，伺间亡归，谓之放鹁鸽 - A local custom where people pretend to sell their wives or concubines, only to escape later, and calls it 'releasing pigeons.'
  • 黄从之，伪为兄妹，鬻于浦东曹氏为妾 - Huang followed this practice, pretending to be siblings, and sold himself to Cao in Pudong as a concubine. Describes the actions taken by Huang.
  • 曹留黄宿于家，翌日将告归，促李出言别 - Cao kept Huang at his home overnight, and the next day, he urged Li to come out and say goodbye. Describes the interactions between the characters.
  • 李始不出，久之乃出，不数语，遽厉声曰 - Li initially did not come out, but after a while, she came out, and without saying much, she suddenly spoke sternly. Describes Li's reaction.
  • 汝鬻我于此，乃谋与我偕遁乎！ - You sold me here, and now you are plotting to escape with me! Expresses Li's accusation.
  • 我至此无返理，汝不速去，我言于主人，缚送官矣 - I have no reason to return here, and if you do not leave quickly, I will tell the master, and he will tie you up and send you to the authorities. Describes Li's threat.
  • 黄大窘，踉跄而归 - Huang was greatly embarrassed and hurriedly returned home. Describes Huang's reaction.
  • 鹁鸽化为黄鹤，一去而不复返矣 - The released pigeon transformed into a yellow crane, and once it left, it never returned. Uses a metaphor to describe the outcome.
  • 此亦为鬼为蜮者之所不自料也 - This is also something that those who act like ghosts and demons could not have anticipated. Concludes the story with a moral judgment.

  • 深觉歉然 - She deeply felt a sense of inadequacy, expressing her feelings.
  • 稍长 - As she grew older, indicating a change in her circumstances.
  • 见母有痰疾 - She saw that her mother had a coughing illness, describing her mother's condition.
  • 发无定时 - It occurred without a fixed schedule, indicating the unpredictability of the illness.
  • 必有人昼夜经心 - There must be someone who cares for her day and night, emphasizing the need for constant care.
  • 方免不测 - To avoid unforeseen dangers, indicating the importance of careful attention.
  • 诚恐一朝远嫁 - She truly feared that one day she would be married far away, expressing her concern.
  • 不得侍奉母疾 - And would not be able to serve her mother's illness, indicating her fear.
  • 而幼字姚氏 - And she was betrothed to the Yao family at a young age, describing her early betrothal.
  • 亲命难违 - It was hard to defy her parents' wishes, indicating the pressure from her family.
  • 无术分身 - She had no way to divide herself, expressing her inability to be in two places at once.
  • 焦思万状 - She was in a state of extreme anxiety, describing her emotional state.
  • 及赘婿后 - After the groomsman arrived, indicating a change in her situation.
  • 欲令别置媵妾 - She wanted to arrange for a concubine, showing her attempt to solve the problem.
  • 以便他日归宁 - So that she could return to her parents' home in the future, indicating her plan.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姑熟 - Gushu, a historical region in present-day Anhui Province, China.
  • 曹氏 - Cao family, a noble family in the Gushu region.
  • 姚氏 - Yao family, a family to which the elder daughter was betrothed.
  • 同治七年 - The seventh year of Tongzhi, referring to the year 1868 during the reign of Emperor Tongzhi.
  • 《金刚经》 - The Diamond Sutra, a Buddhist scripture.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天幸其先已娶妻 - Indicates that fate (天) has already arranged for the person to be married, suggesting a sense of predestination or divine will.
  • 并生子女 - Indicates that the person has already had children, emphasizing the family situation.
  • 众欢遇人不淑 - A common idiom meaning 'many people meet with misfortune,' referring to the general experience of women in marriages that do not go well.
  • 咸讥士也无良 - Means 'all criticize men for being unrighteous,' highlighting societal judgment on men's behavior.
  • 而妹独欣天假之缘 - 'But the sister alone rejoices at the chance provided by heaven,' indicating a positive view of the situation despite societal criticism.
  • 得遂养亲之志 - Means 'to fulfill the wish to care for one's parents,' emphasizing filial piety.
  • 力阻正名之议 - 'Strives to resist the argument for proper naming,' suggesting a refusal to accept social norms or expectations.
  • 安心在家侍奉 - 'Settles down at home to serve,' indicating a decision to stay and care for her parents.
  • 满冀双亲寿享期颐 - 'Fully hopes that both parents will live to a hundred years old,' expressing a desire for long life.
  • 永叙天伦之乐 - 'To always enjoy the joy of family,' emphasizing the importance of family harmony.
  • 何图天降鞠凶 - 'Who would have thought that heaven would bring such misfortune,' indicating unexpected bad news.
  • 慈父见背 - 'The kind father has passed away,' a respectful way to say that the father has died.
  • 丧服未终 - 'The mourning period is not yet over,' indicating that the period of formal mourning has not ended.
  • 老母又染沈疴 - 'The old mother has contracted a serious illness,' indicating that the mother is seriously ill.
  • 殆将不起 - 'Is likely to die,' suggesting that the mother's condition is critical.
  • 呜呼！吾父吾母 - 'Alas! My father and mother,' an expression of deep sorrow.
  • 既违定省于堂前 - 'Have both departed from the hall,' indicating that both parents have left the family home.
  • 生佛生天 - 'May be reborn as a Buddha or ascend to heaven,' a wish for the parents' spiritual elevation.
  • 誓必相从于地下 - 'Swear to follow them in the afterlife,' indicating a promise to join the parents in death.
  • 从此色笑常亲 - 'From now on, their smiles and laughter will always be close,' suggesting a hope for continued happiness in the afterlife.
  • 亦复何恨 - 'There is no more regret,' indicating a sense of peace and acceptance.
  • 惟念在室养亲 - 'But remember that staying at home to care for one's parents,' emphasizing the decision to remain at home.
  • 义非姚氏之人 - 'Is not the duty of a woman from the Yao family,' indicating a rejection of the family's expectations.
  • 生事死殉 - 'To serve in life and follow in death,' indicating a commitment to the parents.
  • 可谓曹门之女 - 'Can be called a daughter of the Cao family,' indicating a sense of identity.
  • 所存原聘金簪二枝 - 'The original betrothal gifts, two gold hairpins,' indicating the return of the betrothal gifts.
  • 望即检还姚氏 - 'Hope to return them to the Yao family immediately,' indicating a desire to sever ties.
  • 永断葛藤 - 'To permanently cut off entanglements,' indicating a desire to end all connections.
  • 以明决绝 - 'To clearly show a final break,' indicating a clear and firm decision.
  • 三妹于父殁之日 - 'The third sister on the day the father died,' indicating the sister's reaction to the father's death.
  • 百计求死不得 - 'Tried every way to die but could not,' indicating the sister's despair.
  • 此番恐亦不免矣 - 'This time, it seems she cannot avoid it,' indicating a fear of the same fate.
  • 哀哉！ - 'Alas!' an expression of deep sorrow.
  • 兄弟叠遭大故 - 'Brothers repeatedly suffer great misfortunes,' indicating repeated tragedies.
  • 双折连枝 - 'Both branches broken together,' a metaphor for the loss of both parents.
  • 悲伤之情，不堪设想 - 'The grief is unbearable to imagine,' indicating extreme sorrow.
  • 窃有一言劝慰者 - 'I have a word of consolation,' indicating a message of comfort.
  • 泉下庭闱 - 'In the afterlife, the family hearth,' indicating the family in the afterlife.
  • 有妹等侍奉 - 'With the sisters serving,' indicating the sisters' care for the parents.
  • 足可放心 - 'It is enough to put one's mind at ease,' indicating reassurance.
  • 兄弟等子职所当尽者 - 'The brothers' duties as sons,' indicating the brothers' responsibilities.
  • 不徒在哀毁二字 - 'Not just in the words of grief and destruction,' indicating that there is more to the role of a son.
  • 但能修身训子 - 'But to cultivate oneself and educate one's children,' indicating a broader responsibility.
  • 积德显亲 - 'To accumulate virtue and honor one's parents,' indicating a moral duty.
  • 自足慰父母之灵 - 'Will be sufficient to comfort the spirits of the parents,' indicating a sense of fulfillment.
  • 而妹等亦从旁含笑矣 - 'And the sisters will also smile from the side,' indicating a sense of shared happiness.
  • 二妹已为孔氏妇 - 'The second sister is now a wife of the Kong family,' indicating her marital status.
  • 有其当尽之职 - 'Has her duties to fulfill,' indicating her responsibilities.
  • 务宜节哀自爱 - 'Must grieve moderately and love oneself,' indicating a need for self-care.
  • 勿蹈非礼 - 'Do not violate proper conduct,' indicating a call to adhere to social norms.
  • 死别须臾 - 'Death is a brief separation,' indicating the fleeting nature of life.
  • 临颖怆然 - 'At the moment of writing, with deep sorrow,' indicating the emotional state of the writer.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天 - Refers to the concept of heaven or fate, often used in classical Chinese to indicate divine will or destiny.
  • 期颐 - Refers to the age of 100, a significant milestone in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 定省 - Refers to the ritual of visiting parents in the morning and evening, a sign of filial piety.
  • 生佛生天 - Refers to the belief in rebirth and spiritual elevation, common in Buddhist and Confucian thought.
  • 泉下 - Refers to the afterlife or the underworld, a common metaphor for death.
  • 庭闱 - Refers to the family hearth or home, symbolizing the family unit.
  • 子职 - Refers to the duties of a son, emphasizing filial piety and responsibility.
  • 积德显亲 - Refers to the practice of accumulating virtue to honor one's parents, a key Confucian value.
  • 非礼 - Refers to behavior that violates social norms or propriety, a concept central to Confucian ethics.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尝梦至一处 - Means 'once dreamed of arriving at a place.'
  • 似大官牙署 - Refers to 'a place resembling a high official's office.'
  • 重门尽掩，阒其无人 - Means 'all doors were closed, and it was empty.'
  • 俄有数人拥一妇自远来 - Means 'soon, several people brought a woman from afar.'
  • 将妇人上下衣服尽去之 - Means 'they removed all her clothes.'
  • 妇犹少艾，微有姿首 - Describes the woman as 'still young and slightly attractive.'
  • 莹然裸立，羞愧之状，殆不可堪 - Means 'she stood naked, her face showing extreme shame and embarrassment.'
  • 贾素负气，直前叱之曰 - Means 'Jia, who was always proud, went forward and scolded them, saying.'
  • 若辈何人，敢肆无礼 - Means 'Who are you people, daring to act so impolitely?'
  • 众微笑曰 - Means 'They smiled and said.'
  • 此何足异 - Means 'What is there to be surprised about?'
  • 言未毕，门忽启 - Means 'Before they finished speaking, the door suddenly opened.'
  • 有数人扛一巨桶出 - Means 'several people carried out a large barrel.'
  • 一吏执文书随其后而去 - Means 'an official carrying documents followed behind and left.'
  • 众即拥裸妇人，贾亦随入 - Means 'The group then took the naked woman, and Jia followed inside.'
  • 历数门，至一广庭 - Means 'They passed through several doors and arrived at a large courtyard.'
  • 见男女数百，或坐，或立，或卧，而皆裸无寸缕 - Means 'They saw hundreds of men and women, some sitting, some standing, some lying down, all naked.'
  • 堂上坐一官，其前设大榨床 - Means 'On the dais sat an official, in front of whom was set up a large press.'
  • 健夫数辈，执大铁叉，任意将男妇叉置槽内 - Means 'Several strong men held large iron forks and placed men and women at will into the slots.'
  • 用大石压榨之，膏血淋漓 - Means 'They pressed them with large stones, and blood and fat flowed everywhere.'
  • 下承以盆，盆满即挹注巨桶中 - Means 'Below, a basin caught the liquid; when it was full, they poured it into the large barrel.'
  • 如是十余次，巨桶乃满 - Means 'This was done over ten times, and the large barrel was finally full.'
  • 数人扛之出 - Means 'Several people carried it out.'
  • 官判文书付一吏，与同出 - Means 'The official handed the documents to an official and left with him.'
  • 贾视吏，乃其已故邻人周达夫也 - Means 'Jia looked at the official and saw that it was his deceased neighbor Zhou Dafu.'
  • 因前呼之 - Means 'So he called out to him.'
  • 周惊曰 - Means 'Zhou was startled and said.'
  • 子胡在此？此岂可久留邪？速从我出 - Means 'Why are you here? This is not a place to stay long. Come out with me quickly.'
  • 贾问桶中何物 - Means 'Jia asked what was in the barrel.'
  • 周曰 - Means 'Zhou said.'
  • 鸦片烟膏也 - Means 'Opium paste.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 绍兴 - Refers to Shaoxing, a city in Zhejiang Province, known for its cultural significance.
  • 老儒 - Refers to an elderly scholar, indicating the respect accorded to learned individuals.
  • 大官牙署 - Refers to a high official's office, suggesting a place of power and authority.
  • 鸦片烟膏 - Refers to opium paste, a highly addictive substance that was prevalent during the Qing Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 绍兴 - The city of Shaoxing.

  • 贾慎庵 - The name of the dreamer.
  • 周达夫 - The name of the deceased neighbor.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 方今承平日久 - Classical expression meaning 'at present, peace has lasted for a long time,' indicating a period of prolonged stability.
  • 生齿繁衍 - Literally 'teeth are numerous,' used idiomatically to mean 'population has grown,' referring to an increase in the number of people.
  • 宜有大劫销除 - Phrase suggesting that a major catastrophe should occur to reduce the population.
  • 贤愚同尽 - Means 'the wise and the foolish perish together,' implying that a great disaster affects everyone equally.
  • 福善祸淫之说 - Refers to the traditional belief that good deeds bring good fortune and evil deeds bring misfortune.
  • 听其人之自取 - Means 'let people take what they deserve,' suggesting a sense of divine justice.
  • 不得归咎于造物之不仁 - Means 'one cannot blame the Creator for being unkind,' indicating that the Creator is just and fair.
  • 罂粟本属草花 - Literally 'poppy is originally a grass flower,' used to describe the natural state of the plant.
  • 其汁淡薄 - Means 'its juice is thin,' describing the quality of the poppy juice before processing.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鸦片烟 - Refers to opium, a substance that became widely used in China during the Qing dynasty but was not yet known in the early Ming period.
  • 上帝 - Refers to the Creator or God, often used in religious contexts.
  • 诸神会议 - Means 'assembly of gods,' suggesting a divine council.
  • 水火刀兵 - Traditional Chinese terms for natural disasters and wars, representing common forms of large-scale destruction.
  • 福善祸淫 - A Confucian concept emphasizing the moral principle that good deeds lead to good outcomes and bad deeds lead to bad outcomes.
  • 造物 - Refers to the Creator or the force that creates and sustains the universe.

Proper Nouns

  • 鸦片烟 - Opium.
  • 上帝 - The Creator or God.
  • 诸神 - Gods or deities.
  • 水火刀兵 - Water, fire, and weapons (representing natural disasters and wars).
  • 福善祸淫 - A Confucian moral principle.
  • 造物 - The Creator or the force that creates and sustains the universe.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 故又命九幽主者 - Formal and somewhat archaic expression indicating a divine or supernatural command.
  • 于无间地狱中 - Refers to the Avici Hell, one of the eight hot hells in Buddhist cosmology, known for its extreme suffering.
  • 择取不忠、不孝、无礼义廉耻诸罪魂 - Selecting those souls guilty of disloyalty, filial impiety, lack of propriety, righteousness, integrity, and shame.
  • 录送此间 - Recording and sending them to this place, indicating a formal process of recording and transferring souls.
  • 榨取膏血 - Extracting marrow and blood, suggesting a process of extracting life force or essence.
  • 转付地上山陵原隰坟衍之神 - Delivering it to the gods of mountains, hills, plains, lowlands, and mounds on earth.
  • 使将此膏血灌入罂粟花根内 - Causing these gods to pour this marrow and blood into the roots of opium poppies.
  • 自根而上达花苞 - From the roots up to the flower buds, indicating the process of the substance moving through the plant.
  • 则其汁自然浓郁 - Then its juice naturally becomes rich and thick.
  • 一经熬炼，光色黝然 - Once refined, it shines with a dark luster.
  • 子试识之 - You should recognize it, suggesting a recognition or acknowledgment of the process.
  • 数十年后，此烟遍天下矣 - In several decades, this smoke will spread throughout the world.
  • 忽又有人驱数十男妇至 - Suddenly, someone drove dozens of men and women to the place, indicating an unexpected arrival.
  • 鞭策甚苦，齐声呼号 - They were whipped and driven very harshly, all crying out together, describing the harsh treatment and collective distress.
  • 贾欲更有所问 - The merchant wanted to ask more questions, indicating a desire for further information.
  • 贾悸而醒，以语人，人无信者 - The merchant was startled awake and told others, but no one believed him, indicating disbelief and skepticism.
  • 至道光中叶后，鸦片烟果盛行 - By the middle of the Daoguang era, opium smoking indeed became widespread, providing a historical context.
  • 而贾已前死矣 - But the merchant had already died before, indicating the merchant's death before the widespread use of opium.
  • 然其语犹在人耳，故其时皆言鸦片烟中有死人膏血 - However, his words still lingered in people's ears, so at that time, it was said that opium contained the marrow and blood of the dead, explaining the origin of a belief.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 九幽主者 - Refers to the ruler of the nine underworlds, a concept in Chinese folklore and religious beliefs.
  • 无间地狱 - Known as the Avici Hell, a place of eternal suffering in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 不忠、不孝、无礼义廉耻 - Moral failings that are considered grave sins in Confucian ethics.
  • 山陵原隰坟衍之神 - Gods associated with natural features such as mountains, hills, plains, lowlands, and mounds, reflecting the animistic beliefs in Chinese culture.
  • 罂粟花 - Opium poppies, which are the source of opium, a highly addictive drug.
  • 道光中叶 - Refers to the middle of the Daoguang era (1821-1850), a period in Chinese history when opium smoking became widespread.
  • 鸦片烟 - Opium smoking, which became a significant social and economic issue in China during the Qing dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 九幽主者 - The ruler of the nine underworlds.
  • 无间地狱 - The Avici Hell.
  • 道光中叶 - The middle of the Daoguang era.
  • 鸦片烟 - Opium smoking.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 君得无于都门市上得之乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Did you not obtain it at the city gate market?' Used to prompt a response or confirmation.
  • 然则仆曾见君夫人 - Means 'Then I have seen your wife,' where '然则' introduces a conclusion based on the preceding question.
  • 因告以吴门舟中相遇事 - Means 'and told him about the encounter in the boat in Wu Gate,' where '因告以' indicates the act of informing.
  • 若然，何相似之甚 - Means 'If so, why is the resemblance so striking?' where '若然' means 'if that is the case.'
  • 此必吾姨也 - Means 'This must be my aunt,' where '必' indicates certainty.
  • 吾妇前一年已物故矣 - Means 'My wife passed away a year ago,' where '物故' is an archaic term for death.
  • 吾妻父生二女 - Means 'My father-in-law had two daughters,' where '妻父' refers to one's father-in-law.
  • 面目相同，虽家人不能辨别 - Means 'They look the same, even family members cannot distinguish between them,' where '面目相同' emphasizes the physical similarity.
  • 长即亡妇，君所见者其妹也 - Means 'The elder is my deceased wife, and the one you saw is her younger sister,' where '长即' means 'the elder is.'
  • 吕因以梦中语告 - Means 'Lü then told him about the dream,' where '因以' indicates the act of recounting.
  • 吾姨固待聘者，当为君作蹇修 - Means 'My aunt was indeed waiting to be married, and I will arrange the match for you,' where '固' means 'indeed' and '蹇修' is a euphemism for matchmaker.
  • 竟宛转媒合之，一时以为佳话 - Means 'Finally, I arranged the marriage through intermediaries, and it became a tale of beauty,' where '宛转' means 'through intermediaries' and '佳话' means 'a beautiful story.'
  • 按《说苑》载齐王起九成之台 - Means 'According to the *Shuo Yuan* (Book of Sayings), King Qi of Qi built the Nine-Tiered Terrace,' where '按' means 'according to' and '说苑' is a historical text.
  • 募国中能画者，赐之钱 - Means 'He recruited talented painters from the country and rewarded them with money,' where '募' means 'recruited' and '赐之钱' means 'rewarded them with money.'
  • 有敬君居常饥寒 - Means 'There was a man named Jing Jun who often suffered from hunger and cold,' where '居常' means 'often' and '饥寒' means 'hunger and cold.'
  • 其妻妙色 - Means 'His wife was beautiful,' where '妙色' emphasizes her beauty.
  • 敬君工画台，贪赐画钱，去家日久 - Means 'Jing Jun was skilled in painting terraces and coveted the reward money, so he was away from home for a long time,' where '工画台' means 'skilled in painting terraces' and '贪赐画钱' means 'coveted the reward money.'
  • 思忆其妻，画像对之而笑 - Means 'He missed his wife and painted her likeness, smiling at it,' where '思忆' means 'missed' and '画像对之而笑' means 'painted her likeness and smiled at it.'
  • 沈所用敬君事即此 - Means 'The story of Jing Jun used by Shen is this,' where '即此' means 'is this.'
  • 然事见《艺文类聚》所引，今本《说苑》无此文也 - Means 'However, this story is found in the *Yiwen Leiju* (Classified Collection of Literature and Art) but not in the current edition of the *Shuo Yuan*,' where '然' means 'however' and '艺文类聚' is another historical text.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 旋侦访得实 - Soon after investigating, the truth was found. '旋' indicates a short time after an action, and '侦访' means 'investigation.'
  • 乃使媒氏告曰 - Then he sent the matchmaker to inform him. '乃' means 'then' or 'thus,' and '媒氏' refers to a matchmaker.
  • 原聘者，吾婿也 - The one who originally betrothed my daughter is my son-in-law. '原聘' means 'originally betrothed.'
  • 他人子安得婿吾女 - How can someone else's son become my daughter's husband? '安得' is an interrogative particle meaning 'how can.'
  • 某翁正&lt；止寺&gt；屠无计 - The old man was at a loss. '正' means 'exactly' or 'just,' and '无计' means 'at a loss.'
  • 而其养子知事且不谐 - But his adopted son knew the matter and it was not harmonious. '养子' means 'adopted son,' and '不谐' means 'not harmonious.'
  • 于人定后怀刃出门 - After the time of human settlement (midnight), he carried a knife and went out. '人定' refers to midnight, and '怀刃' means 'carrying a knife.'
  • 突入妇翁之家 - He suddenly entered his mother-in-law's house. '突入' means 'suddenly entered.'
  • 因左手把其袖，而右手以刃拟之 - He grabbed his sleeve with his left hand, while holding a knife in his right hand and pointing it at him. '把' means 'grab,' and '拟之' means 'pointed it at him.'
  • 尔女不我嫁，吾先杀尔及尔女 - If you do not marry me, I will first kill you and your daughter. '尔女' means 'your daughter,' and '吾先杀尔及尔女' means 'I will first kill you and your daughter.'
  • 归杀婿，吾亦自杀 - Then I will return and kill the groom, and I will also commit suicide. '归杀婿' means 'return and kill the groom.'
  • 四人同日死矣 - All four of us will die on the same day. '四人' means 'four people,' and '同日死矣' means 'will die on the same day.'
  • 妇翁见事急，请如期 - The father-in-law, seeing the urgency, asked to proceed as scheduled. '见事急' means 'seeing the urgency,' and '请如期' means 'asked to proceed as scheduled.'
  • 不能待矣，事在今夕 - It cannot be delayed, the matter is for tonight. '不能待矣' means 'it cannot be delayed,' and '事在今夕' means 'the matter is for tonight.'
  • 然则当释吾入内，略为小女治装 - Then you should let me go inside, and prepare my daughter for a bit. '然则' means 'then,' and '略为小女治装' means 'prepare my daughter for a bit.'
  • 子姑小坐 - You may sit down for a while. '姑' means 'may' or 'please,' and '小坐' means 'sit down for a while.'
  • 谋集健仆缚而送之归 - They planned to gather strong servants to bind and send him back. '谋集' means 'planned to gather,' and '缚而送之归' means 'bind and send him back.'
  • 非计也 - It is not a good plan. '非计也' means 'it is not a good plan.'

  • 养子 - Adopted son, a common practice in traditional Chinese families.

  • 向之称善者，至是而不能无疑 - Literally 'those who previously called him virtuous, by now could not help but doubt,' indicating skepticism about his virtue after his death.

  • 求余为文以释疑 - Literally 'he asked me to write an article to dispel doubts,' indicating a request for clarification.
  • 余乃据《援神契》遭命之说及王充《论衡》福虚祸虚之义 - Literally 'I based my argument on the *Yuan Shenqi* (The Book of Divine Contracts) theory of fate and Wang Chong's *Lunheng* (Critical Essays) discussion of the illusory nature of fortune and misfortune,' explaining the sources of the argument.

  • 然王充所谓福虚祸虚者，余雅不以为然 - Literally 'however, Wang Chong's notion of the illusory nature of fortune and misfortune, I do not agree with,' expressing disagreement with Wang Chong's view.

  • 吾为招童子数人，使子授以经书 - 吾 means 'I,' 为招 means 'to recruit,' 童子 means 'children,' 数人 means 'several,' 使子 means 'make you,' 授以 means 'teach,' and 经书 means 'classics.' This phrase describes the Taoist priest's plan to recruit children to teach them classics.
  • 必有至者 - 必 means 'certainly,' and 有至者 means 'there will be people who come.' This phrase indicates that people will come to learn.
  • 此寒士谋生之恒业也 - 此 means 'this,' 寒士 means 'poor scholar,' 谋生 means 'earn a living,' 之 means 'of,' and 恒业 means 'steady job.' This phrase suggests that teaching is a steady job for poor scholars.
  • 不胜于仰面求人乎 - 不胜 means 'not better than,' 仰面 means 'looking up,' and 求人 means 'asking for help.' This phrase suggests that teaching is better than begging.
  • 李欣然从之 - 李 is a proper name, 欣然 means 'gladly,' and 从之 means 'followed it.' This phrase indicates that Li followed the suggestion.
  • 遂得与邑中士大夫游 - 遂 means 'thus,' 得 means 'able to,' 与 means 'with,' 邑中 means 'in the county,' 士大夫 means 'scholars and officials,' and 游 means 'interact.' This phrase indicates that Li was able to interact with scholars and officials in the county.
  • 有赵翁者，设逆旅以待四方之客 - 有 means 'there is,' 赵翁 is a proper name, 设 means 'set up,' 逆旅 means 'inn,' 以待 means 'to wait for,' and 四方之客 means 'guests from all directions.' This phrase describes Zhao's inn.
  • 家颇小康，而无子 - 家 means 'home,' 颇小康 means 'fairly comfortable,' 而 means 'but,' and 无子 means 'no son.' This phrase describes Zhao's family situation.
  • 止一女，欲为女招婿，而即以为子 - 止 means 'only,' 一女 means 'one daughter,' 欲 means 'want,' 为女招婿 means 'to find a husband for the daughter,' 而 means 'and,' and 即以为子 means 'and consider him as a son.' This phrase describes Zhao's plan to find a husband for his daughter.
  • 久而未得其人 - 久 means 'long,' 而 means 'but,' and 未得其人 means 'did not find the right person.' This phrase indicates that Zhao did not find the right person for a long time.
  • 道士乃说赵翁曰 - 道士 means 'Taoist priest,' 乃 means 'then,' 说 means 'persuade,' 赵翁 is a proper name, and 曰 means 'said.' This phrase indicates that the Taoist priest persuaded Zhao.
  • 翁女若婿本地人，此时虽暂为翁子，久必挈女归耳 - 翁女 means 'your daughter,' 若 means 'if,' 婿 means 'husband,' 本地人 means 'local person,' 此时 means 'at this time,' 虽 means 'although,' 暂为 means 'temporarily be,' 翁子 means 'your son,' 久 means 'long,' 必 means 'certainly,' 挈女 means 'take the daughter,' and 归耳 means 'back home.' This phrase suggests that if the husband is a local, he will eventually take the daughter back home.
  • 李氏子，无家者也，孑然一身，流落千里，若为翁婿，必长为翁子矣 - 李氏子 means 'Li's son,' 无家者 means 'without a family,' 孑然一身 means 'alone,' 流落 means 'wandered,' 千里 means 'thousands of miles,' 若 means 'if,' 为翁婿 means 'become your son-in-law,' 必 means 'certainly,' 长 means 'long,' 为翁子 means 'be your son,' and 矣 means 'already.' This phrase suggests that Li, being alone and wandering, would stay as a son if he became Zhao's son-in-law.
  • 赵翁韪其言，乃赘李于家 - 赵翁 is a proper name, 韪 means 'approve,' 其言 means 'his words,' 乃 means 'then,' 赘 means 'marry into,' 李 is a proper name, and 于家 means 'into the family.' This phrase indicates that Zhao approved and married Li into his family.
  • 久之，谓赵翁曰 - 久之 means 'after a long time,' 谓 means 'told,' 赵翁 is a proper name, and 曰 means 'said.' This phrase indicates that after a long time, Li told Zhao.
  • 某视此间庠序中人，其文艺亦与某等耳 - 某 means 'I,' 视 means 'see,' 此间 means 'here,' 庠序 means 'schools,' 中人 means 'people,' 其 means 'their,' 文艺 means 'literary and artistic skills,' 亦 means 'also,' 与 means 'with,' and 某等 means 'me.' This phrase suggests that Li's skills are comparable to those of the local scholars.
  • 使得与试，一衿非所难也 - 使得 means 'if allowed,' 与 means 'to,' 试 means 'take the exam,' 一衿 means 'a scholar's robe,' 非 means 'not,' 所难 means 'difficult.' This phrase suggests that if allowed to take the exam, obtaining a scholar's robe would not be difficult.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 将死前数日，范桂芳附其体，与沈氏妇女相问答 - Means 'several days before she died, Fan Guifang's spirit attached to her body and conversed with the Shen family women.'
  • 盖范生前曾以钱债事与沈氏有隙也 - Means 'because Fan had a dispute with the Shen family over money matters while he was alive.'
  • 范故杭人侨寓临平者 - Means 'Fan was originally from Hangzhou but had settled in Linping.'
  • 故其语犹杂杭音，鬼语亦然 - Means 'thus his speech still carried a Hangzhou accent, and so did his ghostly voice.'
  • 溯范之死，四十余年矣 - Means 'it has been over forty years since Fan's death.'
  • 溯其生年，则已在百岁外 - Means 'tracing back to his birth, he would be over a hundred years old.'
  • 范本一小人 - Means 'Fan was originally a small-minded person.'

  • 鬼神之事，真有不可知者 - Means 'the matter of ghosts and spirits truly is something that cannot be known.'
  • 古者庙焉而人鬼享 - Means 'in ancient times, temples were built where people and ghosts could enjoy offerings.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 未知生，焉知死？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'If one does not know life, how can one know death?' It emphasizes the priority of understanding life before exploring death.
  • 未能事人，焉能事鬼？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'If one cannot serve people, how can one serve spirits?' It underscores the importance of human relationships before spiritual matters.
  • 蓋孔子亦有所不知耳 - Commentary suggesting that even Confucius had his limits of knowledge. The phrase '蓋' introduces an explanatory statement.
  • 非原始，而知所以生；不能反終，而知所以死 - Complex statement suggesting that one must understand the origin of life to know how to live and the end of life to know how to die. The phrase '非...而' introduces a contrastive relationship.
  • 原始反終，故知死生之說 - Statement meaning 'By understanding the origin and the end, one knows the principles of life and death.' The phrase '故' introduces a causal relationship.
  • 夫人之生也，由父精母血感而成胎 - Statement explaining the biological process of conception, emphasizing the role of both parents in the creation of life.
  • 夫人而知之矣 - Concluding remark suggesting that this biological fact is well-known or obvious.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孔子 - Confucius, a renowned Chinese philosopher and teacher, whose teachings and sayings are foundational in Chinese culture and philosophy.
  • 子路 - Zilu, one of Confucius' most prominent disciples, known for his loyalty and bravery.
  • 紫阳氏 - Refers to Zhu Xi, a prominent Neo-Confucian scholar and philosopher who lived during the Song Dynasty. He is known for his extensive commentaries on Confucian texts.
  • 生 - Refers to life, a fundamental concept in Chinese philosophy and religion.
  • 死 - Refers to death, another fundamental concept in Chinese philosophy and religion.
  • 事人 - Serving or dealing with people, emphasizing the importance of human relationships.
  • 事鬼 - Serving or dealing with spirits, emphasizing the spiritual aspect of life.
  • 父精母血 - Refers to the biological components of conception, emphasizing the role of both parents in the creation of life.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The rhetorical structure of this question is straightforward but emphasizes the logical sequence of understanding life before death.
  • Similarly, this question emphasizes the logical sequence of serving people before spirits.
  • The phrase '蓋' introduces an explanatory statement, which needs to be rendered clearly in English.
  • The contrastive structure '非...而' needs to be preserved to maintain the logical relationship between the origin and the end of life.
  • The causal relationship introduced by '故' needs to be preserved to maintain the logical flow of the argument.
  • The biological process described here is straightforward but needs to be rendered in a way that is clear and accurate in English.

Proper Nouns

  • 孔子 - Confucius.
  • 子路 - Zilu.
  • 紫阳氏 - Zhu Xi.
  • 父 - Father.
  • 母 - Mother.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 三部、九候 - Terms from Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) referring to pulse diagnosis. '三部' refers to the three sections of the body (upper, middle, lower) where the pulse is felt, and '九候' refers to the nine pulses (three in each section).
  • 懸壺之客 - Metaphor for doctors, derived from the legend of a doctor who hung a gourd outside his door as a sign of his practice.
  • 衙推之名，被之屠酤 - Criticism of the misuse of titles, where '衙推' (a title for a low-ranking official) is given to butchers and wine sellers, implying a degradation of status.
  • 黃帝 - Refers to the legendary Yellow Emperor, a central figure in Chinese mythology and the supposed author of many ancient texts, including the *Huangdi Neijing* (*Yellow Emperor's Inner Canon*).
  • 周官 - Refers to the *Zhouli* (*Rites of Zhou*), an ancient Chinese text that describes the organization of the Zhou Dynasty government.
  • 卜師 - Refers to diviners or fortune-tellers.
  • 廢龜之論 - Refers to a theory or argument about abandoning the use of tortoise shells for divination, attributed to the Tang Dynasty scholar Li Hua.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《素問》 - The *Suwen* is one of the two main parts of the *Huangdi Neijing* (*Yellow Emperor's Inner Canon*), a foundational text in TCM.
  • 黃帝 - The Yellow Emperor is a legendary figure in Chinese history, often credited with the creation of various aspects of Chinese culture, including medicine.
  • 周官 - The *Zhouli* is an ancient text that details the administrative structure of the Zhou Dynasty, including the roles of various officials.
  • 卜師 - Diviners were important figures in ancient Chinese society, often consulted for guidance on important matters.
  • 李華 - Li Hua (715–766) was a Tang Dynasty scholar and poet known for his critical views on certain traditional practices, including the use of tortoise shells for divination.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence raises questions about the consistency of the terms '三部' and '九候' in modern practice, suggesting that their meanings may have changed over time.
  • This sentence contrasts the decline of traditional pulse diagnosis with the widespread presence of doctors and the misuse of titles.
  • This sentence emphasizes the historical and authoritative nature of TCM, tracing its origins to the Yellow Emperor and its inclusion in the *Zhouli*.
  • This sentence argues that the lack of medical care can lead to moral failings and shortened lives, emphasizing the importance of medicine in fulfilling familial duties.
  • This sentence discusses the historical roles of doctors and diviners in the *Zhouli* and the critical view of divination expressed by Li Hua.

Proper Nouns

  • 黃帝 - The Yellow Emperor.
  • 周官 - The *Zhouli*.
  • 李華 - Li Hua, a Tang Dynasty scholar and poet.

  • 是仍复其初矣 - The man's realization that the diviner's advice has returned to the original orientation.
  • 自夏徂冬，我奉彼为蓍龟，乃颠倒如此乎 - The man's frustration with the diviner's inconsistent advice.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 今舍此无他法，而尔执不可，我先死尔前矣 - Statement of extreme resolve, suggesting that the speaker will take their own life if the listener does not comply.

  • 自来表扬节妇者，必题以节孝二字 - Statement about how virtuous women are traditionally praised with the titles of 'chastity' and 'filial piety.'
  • 盖女子奉父母之命，以身事人，不负其夫，即是不负其父母，故节即孝也 - Philosophical statement explaining that a woman's fidelity to her husband is equivalent to filial piety towards her parents.
  • 乃如此女，则节孝不能两全 - Statement indicating that in this case, the woman could not fulfill both her duty of chastity and filial piety.
  • 是亦居人伦之变矣 - Phrase suggesting that this situation is an exception to the usual human relationships.
  • 女子为未婚夫守义 - Phrase referring to a woman maintaining her loyalty to an未婚夫 (betrothed husband).
  • 历来议论，颇有异同 - Phrase indicating that there have been differing opinions on this matter throughout history.
  • 此女改归他族，于礼本无不可 - Statement suggesting that it is not against the rules for this woman to remarry another family.
  • 使其执志不从，父死而母随之，此恨岂有极欤 - Rhetorical question expressing the depth of sorrow if the woman were to refuse and her mother also died.
  • 勉从亲命以全孝 - Phrase indicating that the woman complied with her parents' wishes to fulfill her filial duty.
  • 而又郁郁以死，则仍所以徇其节 - Statement explaining that her subsequent depression and death were a way of maintaining her chastity.
  • 君子于此女无讥焉 - Phrase indicating that a gentleman would not criticize this woman.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 未婚夫 - Refers to a betrothed husband, a concept that was important in arranged marriages in traditional Chinese society.
  • 人伦 - Refers to the moral and social order of human relationships, particularly within the family.

  • 里党闻之，咸叹殷翁高义而又有远见 - The villagers all praised Mr. Yin for his noble character and foresight; '里党' (villagers) refers to the local community.

  • 俾阿九从塾师读 - Means 'and made Ajiu study under a private teacher', where 俾 (bǐ) means 'to make' or 'to cause', and 塾师 (shú shī) means 'private teacher'.
  • 俄而贼平 - Means 'soon after the bandits were defeated', where 俄而 (é ér) means 'soon after'.
  • 乔自蓄钱偿倡家赎身 - Means 'Qiao saved up money to repay the brothel and buy her freedom', where 自蓄钱 (zì xù qián) means 'saved up money', and 赎身 (shú shēn) means 'buy freedom'.
  • 挈阿九归松江 - Means 'and took Ajiu back to Songjiang', where 挈 (qiè) means 'to take with', and 归 (guī) means 'to return'.
  • 从其兄弟以居 - Means 'and lived with her brothers', where 从 (cóng) means 'to follow' or 'to live with', and 居 (jū) means 'to live'.
  • 阿九长，为娶妇 - Means 'when Ajiu grew up, Qiao arranged for him to marry', where 长 (zhǎng) means 'grew up', and 为娶妇 (wéi qǔ fù) means 'arranged for him to marry'.
  • 乃复设祭拜舅姑与夫曰 - Means 'she then set up an altar to worship her in-laws and husband, saying', where 设祭 (shè jì) means 'set up an altar', and 舅姑 (jiù gū) refers to in-laws.
  • 曩奉命存孤 - Means 'I was once entrusted with the task of caring for an orphan', where 曩 (nǎng) means 'once' or 'in the past', and 存孤 (cún gū) means 'caring for an orphan'.
  • 幸不辱命 - Means 'fortunately, I did not fail my mission', where 幸 (xìng) means 'fortunately', and 辱命 (rǔ mìng) means 'fail my mission'.
  • 然妇人究以节为重 - Means 'but for women, chastity is the most important thing', where 究 (jiū) means 'ultimately' or 'in the end', and 节 (jié) means 'chastity'.
  • 我一妇人 - Means 'I, a woman', where 一 (yī) is a particle used for emphasis.
  • 始为贼贞人 - Means 'first, I was a chaste woman', where 始 (shǐ) means 'first', and 贞人 (zhēn rén) means 'chaste woman'.

Idiomatic Expressions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 上与太清为体 - Means 'to be in harmony with the Great Void (太清)', referring to the purest, most ethereal state of being.
  • 赫然森列而为明神 - Vivid expression meaning 'standing out clearly and in an orderly array as bright deities', used to describe the manifestation of divine beings.
  • 其品愈下，则浊气愈多 - Means 'the lower their rank, the more impure qi they have', indicating a correlation between spiritual rank and purity of qi.
  • 去人亦益近 - Means 'the closer they are to humans', suggesting a proximity to the human realm.
  • 生本凡庸，死亦冗 - Means 'born ordinary, and dying without significance', describing the fate of common people.
  • 依其子孙以居 - Means 'relying on their descendants to reside', referring to the belief that spirits linger near their descendants.
  • 汝平时所一召而即至者 - Means 'those who you summon and immediately appear', referring to the spirits that respond to calls.
  • 其气愈沉愈下，堕入九幽 - Means 'their qi becomes heavier and sinks deeper, falling into the Nine Lower Regions', describing the descent of impure spirits.
  • 清气久绝，纯乎浊气 - Means 'having lost their pure qi for a long time, being purely impure qi', describing the state of extremely evil people.
  • 有恩报恩，有怨报怨 - Means 'to repay kindness with kindness, and to repay resentment with resentment', referring to the principle of retribution.
  • 其心既有所系著 - Means 'their hearts are attached to something', indicating a state of attachment or obsession.
  • 不能上升 - Means 'unable to ascend', referring to the inability to reach higher spiritual realms.
  • 往往能见形为祸福 - Means 'often able to manifest and bring about good or evil', describing the ability of spirits to influence the world.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 九幽 - Refers to the Nine Lower Regions, the deepest and darkest parts of the underworld in Chinese cosmology.
  • 匹夫匹妇 - Refers to ordinary people, emphasizing the commonality of the subjects discussed.
  • 士大夫 - Refers to scholars and officials, often associated with the educated and elite classes in Chinese society.
  • 易箦 - Refers to the act of changing one's mat, symbolizing the transition from life to death in Chinese culture.
  • 巫觋 - Refers to shamans or diviners, practitioners of traditional Chinese spiritual practices.

  • 有恩报恩，有怨报怨 - Refers to the principle of retribution, a common theme in Chinese moral and spiritual thought.
